title: Bristol Myers Squibb Building Life Science Industry’s Most Advanced AI Factory on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
excerpt: The pharmaceutical company has demonstrated how AI can transform how drugs discovery and is now scaling AI across R&D to accelerate the discovery of next-generation medicines.
content_html:
Erin Davis calls it the “SuperDuperPOD.” That’s two things in one name: pharmaceutical giant Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) already runs one of the largest AI clusters in life sciences, with serious results to show for it. And they’re doubling down.

BMS announced today it is deploying its second NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, this one built on eight DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 systems — the most powerful and energy-efficient AI cluster in life sciences.

“Instead of equipping a small group of researchers with access to the  supercomputer, we’re opening it up to literally every scientist,” says Davis, vice president of research business insights and technology at BMS. “No one has to wait, and no one is told they have a limit.”

The eight rack-scale systems, each comprising NVIDIA Vera CPUs and Rubin GPUs, deliver up to 10x the performance per megawatt of the infrastructure it replaces. It will give researchers at the global pharmaceutical giant access to a unified AI platform — including NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit for biological AI — for running predictions, training models and powering agentic workflows across the full drug discovery pipeline.

What Davis and other top BMS researchers are really after is what that access makes possible: faster cycles, bigger chemical spaces and a full drug discovery pipeline where researchers think about the science, not the logistics of lining up resources.

The mandate, says Payal Sheth — a scientist who spent her career inside drug discovery labs before taking on an expanded role in January as senior vice president of therapeutic discovery sciences at BMS — is moving from “sort of this abstract position of what AI can do to actually translating that to measurable impact.”

BMS has operated a DGX SuperPOD for about three years, producing meaningful results. AI-enabled target identification already saves scientists weeks of manual work, freeing time to focus on the highest-value scientific decisions. BMS’s team has used AI to expand its library of CELMoD compounds — molecules engineered to selectively degrade cancer-causing proteins, with applications in blood cancer treatment and beyond. This has opened the door to new targets and new potential medicines across a wider range of diseases. AI is also applied in lead optimization stages of drug discovery using a methodology Sheth calls “Predict First,” which informs experimental gating based on design predictions.

“We use predictions as a way to prioritize synthesis of molecules with multi parameter optimization,” she explains, “to weed out molecules that wouldn’t necessarily meet the property landscape we’re working towards. This ensures precious laboratory experiments are aligned with progressing molecules that have the highest probability of success.

These research AI applications have significant impact on compute needs across the research organization.“We’re saturated,” Davis says. “We’re in production with some very large-scale predictions around large molecules. We’re building our own foundational models, and that takes a lot of GPUs.”

With the new system coming, Davis already has her pitch for researchers thinking about where to do their best work: “Welcome to Limitless Compute.”

A computational chemist by training, Davis spent years doing the science before concluding the technology wasn’t keeping up — and that she’d rather go fix it. She spent roughly 15 years on the vendor side, building enterprise platforms at ChemAxon, Schrödinger and X-Chem. At every company, pharma was wrestling with the same bottleneck.

“It’s not the technology,” she says. “The challenge is how to get that into the hands of actual scientists and learn from it.”

She knows what’s at stake personally. Her father died five years ago, she says, “a very horrible death of Alzheimer’s.” BMS has a significant investment in brain health — a notoriously hard area. “Even if he was still going to die,” Davis says, “if there was symptom remediation along the way, it would have saved suffering for everybody in the family. Dementia is especially cruel.”

Davis’s team is combining the existing DGX SuperPOD and the new DGX Vera Rubin NVL72-powered system into a unified environment — a single data plane, accessible from every BMS site globally. 

Barriers that made the earlier system hard to reach — site-specific restrictions left over from past acquisitions, the need for deep computational expertise — are being replaced with AI-native tooling managed through NVIDIA Mission Control. Researchers will be able to initiate complex predictions in plain English.

“The compute infrastructure is what connects all of our scientists together and ensures that our learnings are institutionalized,” Sheth explains. Datasets from a program run in Lawrenceville, New Jersey, feed models that a team in San Diego, California, can draw on. The learnings “can be applied in context of any program we work on.”

“There’s a cumulative learning loop today in drug discovery that did not exist when I first started my career,” Sheth explains. “Every project was treated differently, and there were discrete sets of learnings that did not compound into any kind of intelligence framework within discovery.”

Today, BMS is using AI to expand that learning loop into a discovery system where every experiment, clinical readout, and partnership compounds into higher-conviction scientific decisions, faster. 

Agentic workflows can further enhance the architecture of R&D.

“Agents don’t care,” Davis says. “They go all across. And that is a huge game-changer because now we can learn from decisions across the silos and across programs.”

“When you as a scientist can go to an army of well-vetted, fully trained virtual scientists that have BMS knowledge baked in now you’re a whole team in and of yourself.”

Human instincts, Sheth says, aren’t replaced, “they’re augmented with more quantitative insights and predictions.” The ability to scale that with compute, she says, “is where the excitement of the impact of AI is going to be fully realized.”

“You still have to have that human brain driving things,” Davis adds, “still looking for caveats and gotchas, still teaching them how to utilize knowledge. But this takes up the capabilities of individual humans substantially.”

Davis says the new system has a plan already mapped to it: a detailed allocation across modalities, from small and large molecule design to clinical applications to digital twins. “We didn’t just buy this to have the biggest compute,” she says. “The SuperDuperPOD is basically at every node along the way.”

When BMS Chief Digital and Technology Officer Greg Meyers asked Davis whether she was sure she could even saturate the SuperDuperPOD, her answer was direct.

“Just give us time,” she told him.

Featured image credit: Bristol Myers Squibb

blocks.html_en: Erin Davis calls it the “SuperDuperPOD.” That’s two things in one name: pharmaceutical giant Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) already runs one of the largest AI clusters in life sciences, with serious results to show for it. And they’re doubling down.

sentences.en: Erin Davis calls it the “SuperDuperPOD.”
sentences.zh: 艾琳·戴维斯 (Erin Davis) 称之为“SuperDuperPOD”。

sentences.en: That’s two things in one name: pharmaceutical giant Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) already runs one of the largest AI clusters in life sciences, with serious results to show for it.
sentences.zh: 这是一物两用：制药巨头百时美施贵宝 (BMS) 已经运营着生命科学领域最大的人工智能集群之一，并取得了丰硕的成果。

sentences.en: And they’re doubling down.
sentences.zh: 他们正在加倍努力。

blocks.html_en: BMS announced today it is deploying its second NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, this one built on eight DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 systems — the most powerful and energy-efficient AI cluster in life sciences.

sentences.en: BMS announced today it is deploying its second NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, this one built on eight DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 systems — the most powerful and energy-efficient AI cluster in life sciences.
sentences.zh: BMS 今天宣布正在部署第二个 NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD，该系统基于八个 DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 系统构建，这是生命科学领域最强大、最节能的 AI 集群。

blocks.html_en: “Instead of equipping a small group of researchers with access to the  supercomputer, we’re opening it up to literally every scientist,” says Davis, vice president of research business insights and technology at BMS. “No one has to wait, and no one is told they have a limit.”

sentences.en: “Instead of equipping a small group of researchers with access to the supercomputer, we’re opening it up to literally every scientist,” says Davis, vice president of research business insights and technology at BMS.
sentences.zh: BMS 负责研究业务洞察和技术的副总裁 Davis 表示：“我们不是为一小群研究人员提供超级计算机的访问权限，而是向几乎所有科学家开放。”

sentences.en: “No one has to wait, and no one is told they have a limit.”
sentences.zh: “没有人需要等待，也没有人被告知他们有限制。”

blocks.html_en: The eight rack-scale systems, each comprising NVIDIA Vera CPUs and Rubin GPUs, deliver up to 10x the performance per megawatt of the infrastructure it replaces. It will give researchers at the global pharmaceutical giant access to a unified AI platform — including NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit for biological AI — for running predictions, training models and powering agentic workflows across the full drug discovery pipeline.

sentences.en: The eight rack-scale systems, each comprising NVIDIA Vera CPUs and Rubin GPUs, deliver up to 10x the performance per megawatt of the infrastructure it replaces.
sentences.zh: 八个机架级系统均包含 NVIDIA Vera CPU 和 Rubin GPU，其每兆瓦性能比其所取代的基础设施高出 10 倍。

sentences.en: It will give researchers at the global pharmaceutical giant access to a unified AI platform — including NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit for biological AI — for running predictions, training models and powering agentic workflows across the full drug discovery pipeline.
sentences.zh: 它将让这家全球制药巨头的研究人员能够访问统一的 AI 平台（包括用于生物 AI 的 NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit），以便在整个药物发现流程中运行预测、训练模型并为代理工作流程提供动力。

blocks.html_en: What Davis and other top BMS researchers are really after is what that access makes possible: faster cycles, bigger chemical spaces and a full drug discovery pipeline where researchers think about the science, not the logistics of lining up resources.

sentences.en: What Davis and other top BMS researchers are really after is what that access makes possible: faster cycles, bigger chemical spaces and a full drug discovery pipeline where researchers think about the science, not the logistics of lining up resources.
sentences.zh: Davis 和其他 BMS 顶尖研究人员真正追求的是这种访问所带来的可能性：更快的周期、更大的化学空间和完整的药物发现管道，研究人员在其中思考科学，而不是排列资源的后勤工作。

blocks.html_en: The mandate, says Payal Sheth — a scientist who spent her career inside drug discovery labs before taking on an expanded role in January as senior vice president of therapeutic discovery sciences at BMS — is moving from “sort of this abstract position of what AI can do to actually translating that to measurable impact.”

sentences.en: The mandate, says Payal Sheth — a scientist who spent her career inside drug discovery labs before taking on an expanded role in January as senior vice president of therapeutic discovery sciences at BMS — is moving from “sort of this abstract position of what AI can do to actually translating that to measurable impact.”
sentences.zh: Payal Sheth 是一位科学家，她的职业生涯都在药物发现实验室度过，随后于 1 月份担任 BMS 治疗发现科学高级副总裁，她表示，这项任务正在从“人工智能可以做什么的这种抽象立场，转变为实际将其转化为可衡量的影响”。

This has opened the door to new targets and new potential medicines across a wider range of diseases. AI is also applied in lead optimization stages of drug discovery using a methodology Sheth calls “Predict First,” which informs experimental gating based on design predictions.

sentences.zh: BMS 运营 DGX SuperPOD 大约三年了，取得了有意义的成果。

sentences.en: AI-enabled target identification already saves scientists weeks of manual work, freeing time to focus on the highest-value scientific decisions.
sentences.zh: 支持人工智能的目标识别已经为科学家节省了数周的手动工作时间，让他们有时间专注于最高价值的科学决策。

sentences.en: BMS’s team has used AI to expand its library of CELMoD compounds — molecules engineered to selectively degrade cancer-causing proteins, with applications in blood cancer treatment and beyond.
sentences.zh: BMS 的团队利用 AI 扩展了 CELMoD 化合物库，这些化合物是经过工程改造的分子，可选择性降解致癌蛋白质，可应用于血癌治疗等领域。

sentences.en: This has opened the door to new targets and new potential medicines across a wider range of diseases.
sentences.zh: 这为治疗更广泛疾病的新靶点和新的潜在药物打开了大门。

sentences.en: AI is also applied in lead optimization stages of drug discovery using a methodology Sheth calls “Predict First,” which informs experimental gating based on design predictions.
sentences.zh: 人工智能还应用于药物发现的先导化合物优化阶段，使用 Sheth 称之为“预测优先”的方法，该方法根据设计预测为实验门控提供信息。

Image credit: Bristol Myers Squibb</figcaption>

sentences.en: Image credit: Bristol Myers Squibb
sentences.zh: 图片来源：百时美施贵宝

sentences.zh: “今天的药物发现有一个累积的学习循环，而我刚开始职业生涯时并不存在，”谢思解释道。

sentences.en: “Every project was treated differently, and there were discrete sets of learnings that did not compound into any kind of intelligence framework within discovery.”
sentences.zh: “每个项目都受到不同的对待，并且存在一些离散的学习集，这些学习集不会复合到发现中的任何类型的情报框架中。”

blocks.html_en: Today, BMS is using AI to expand that learning loop into a discovery system where every experiment, clinical readout, and partnership compounds into higher-conviction scientific decisions, faster.

sentences.en: Today, BMS is using AI to expand that learning loop into a discovery system where every experiment, clinical readout, and partnership compounds into higher-conviction scientific decisions, faster.
sentences.zh: 如今，BMS 正在利用人工智能将学习循环扩展为一个发现系统，在该系统中，每个实验、临床读数和合作伙伴关系都会更快地转化为更有说服力的科学决策。

blocks.html_en: Agentic workflows can further enhance the architecture of R&D.

sentences.en: Agentic workflows can further enhance the architecture of R&D.
sentences.zh: 代理工作流程可以进一步增强研发架构。

blocks.html_en: “Agents don’t care,” Davis says. “They go all across. And that is a huge game-changer because now we can learn from decisions across the silos and across programs.”

sentences.en: “Agents don’t care,” Davis says.
sentences.zh: “特工们不在乎，”戴维斯说。

sentences.en: “They go all across.
sentences.zh: “他们到处走。

sentences.en: And that is a huge game-changer because now we can learn from decisions across the silos and across programs.”
sentences.zh: 这是一个巨大的游戏规则改变者，因为现在我们可以从各个部门和各个项目的决策中学习。”

blocks.html_en: “When you as a scientist can go to an army of well-vetted, fully trained virtual scientists that have BMS knowledge baked in now you’re a whole team in and of yourself.”

sentences.en: “When you as a scientist can go to an army of well-vetted, fully trained virtual scientists that have BMS knowledge baked in now you’re a whole team in and of yourself.”
sentences.zh: “当你作为一名科学家可以加入一支由经过严格审查、训练有素的虚拟科学家组成的大军并融入 BMS 知识时，现在你就是一个完整的团队了。”

blocks.html_en: Human instincts, Sheth says, aren’t replaced, “they’re augmented with more quantitative insights and predictions.” The ability to scale that with compute, she says, “is where the excitement of the impact of AI is going to be fully realized.”

sentences.en: Human instincts, Sheth says, aren’t replaced, “they’re augmented with more quantitative insights and predictions.”
sentences.zh: 谢思说，人类的本能并没有被取代，“它们被更多的定量见解和预测所增强。”

sentences.en: The ability to scale that with compute, she says, “is where the excitement of the impact of AI is going to be fully realized.”
sentences.zh: 她说，通过计算来扩展这一点的能力“将充分实现人工智能影响的令人兴奋的地方。”

blocks.html_en: “You still have to have that human brain driving things,” Davis adds, “still looking for caveats and gotchas, still teaching them how to utilize knowledge. But this takes up the capabilities of individual humans substantially.”

sentences.en: “You still have to have that human brain driving things,” Davis adds, “still looking for caveats and gotchas, still teaching them how to utilize knowledge.
sentences.zh: “你仍然需要用人脑来驱动事物，”戴维斯补充道，“仍然在寻找警告和陷阱，仍然教他们如何利用知识。

sentences.en: But this takes up the capabilities of individual humans substantially.”
sentences.zh: 但这极大地占用了人类个体的能力。”

blocks.html_en: Davis says the new system has a plan already mapped to it: a detailed allocation across modalities, from small and large molecule design to clinical applications to digital twins. “We didn’t just buy this to have the biggest compute,” she says. “The SuperDuperPOD is basically at every node along the way.”

sentences.en: Davis says the new system has a plan already mapped to it: a detailed allocation across modalities, from small and large molecule design to clinical applications to digital twins.
sentences.zh: 戴维斯表示，新系统已经制定了一个计划：跨模式的详细分配，从小分子和大分子设计到临床应用再到数字双胞胎。

sentences.en: “We didn’t just buy this to have the biggest compute,” she says.
sentences.zh: “我们购买它不仅仅是为了拥有最大的计算能力，”她说。

sentences.en: “The SuperDuperPOD is basically at every node along the way.”
sentences.zh: “SuperDuperPOD 基本上遍布整个过程中的每个节点。”

blocks.html_en: When BMS Chief Digital and Technology Officer Greg Meyers asked Davis whether she was sure she could even saturate the SuperDuperPOD, her answer was direct.

sentences.en: When BMS Chief Digital and Technology Officer Greg Meyers asked Davis whether she was sure she could even saturate the SuperDuperPOD, her answer was direct.
sentences.zh: 当 BMS 首席数字和技术官格雷格·迈耶斯 (Greg Meyers) 询问戴维斯是否确定她可以让 SuperDuperPOD 达到饱和时，她的回答很直接。

blocks.html_en: “Just give us time,” she told him.

sentences.en: “Just give us time,” she told him.
sentences.zh: “请给我们时间，”她告诉他。

blocks.html_en: Featured image credit: Bristol Myers Squibb

sentences.en: Featured image credit: Bristol Myers Squibb
sentences.zh: 特色图片来源：百时美施贵宝

content_html_zh:
艾琳·戴维斯 (Erin Davis) 称之为“SuperDuperPOD”。 这是一物两用：制药巨头百时美施贵宝 (BMS) 已经运营着生命科学领域最大的人工智能集群之一，并取得了丰硕的成果。 他们正在加倍努力。

BMS 今天宣布正在部署第二个 NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD，该系统基于八个 DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 系统构建，这是生命科学领域最强大、最节能的 AI 集群。

BMS 负责研究业务洞察和技术的副总裁 Davis 表示：“我们不是为一小群研究人员提供超级计算机的访问权限，而是向几乎所有科学家开放。” “没有人需要等待，也没有人被告知他们有限制。”

八个机架级系统均包含 NVIDIA Vera CPU 和 Rubin GPU，其每兆瓦性能比其所取代的基础设施高出 10 倍。 它将让这家全球制药巨头的研究人员能够访问统一的 AI 平台（包括用于生物 AI 的 NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit），以便在整个药物发现流程中运行预测、训练模型并为代理工作流程提供动力。

Davis 和其他 BMS 顶尖研究人员真正追求的是这种访问所带来的可能性：更快的周期、更大的化学空间和完整的药物发现管道，研究人员在其中思考科学，而不是排列资源的后勤工作。

Payal Sheth 是一位科学家，她的职业生涯都在药物发现实验室度过，随后于 1 月份担任 BMS 治疗发现科学高级副总裁，她表示，这项任务正在从“人工智能可以做什么的这种抽象立场，转变为实际将其转化为可衡量的影响”。

BMS 运营 DGX SuperPOD 大约三年了，取得了有意义的成果。 支持人工智能的目标识别已经为科学家节省了数周的手动工作时间，让他们有时间专注于最高价值的科学决策。 BMS 的团队利用 AI 扩展了 CELMoD 化合物库，这些化合物是经过工程改造的分子，可选择性降解致癌蛋白质，可应用于血癌治疗等领域。 这为治疗更广泛疾病的新靶点和新的潜在药物打开了大门。 人工智能还应用于药物发现的先导化合物优化阶段，使用 Sheth 称之为“预测优先”的方法，该方法根据设计预测为实验门控提供信息。

“我们使用预测作为通过多参数优化优先考虑分子合成的方法，”她解释道，“以淘汰不一定符合我们正在努力的性能前景的分子。 这确保了宝贵的实验室实验与具有最高成功概率的进展分子保持一致。

这些研究人工智能应用对整个研究组织的计算需求产生了重大影响。“我们已经饱和了，”戴维斯说。 “我们正在对大分子进行一些非常大规模的预测。 我们正在构建自己的基础模型，这需要大量 GPU。”

随着新系统的到来，戴维斯已经向研究人员提出了她的建议，让他们思考在哪里可以发挥最大的作用：“欢迎来到无限计算。”

戴维斯是一名训练有素的计算化学家，她花了数年时间进行科学研究，然后才得出结论，技术已经跟不上——她宁愿去修复它。 她在供应商方面工作了大约 15 年，在 ChemAxon、Schrödinger 和 X-Chem 构建企业平台。 每家公司的制药公司都面临着同样的瓶颈。

“这不是技术，”她说。 “挑战在于如何将其交到真正的科学家手中并从中学习。”

她个人知道什么是利害攸关的。 她说，她的父亲五年前去世了，“死于阿尔茨海默氏症，非常可怕。” BMS 在大脑健康方面投入了大量资金——这是一个众所周知的困难领域。 “即使他仍然会死，”戴维斯说，“如果一路上有症状治疗，这也会为家庭中的每个人减轻痛苦。 痴呆症尤其残酷。”

Davis 的团队正在将现有的 DGX SuperPOD 和新的 DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 支持的系统整合到一个统一的环境中——一个可从全球每个 BMS 站点访问的单一数据平面。

早期系统难以实现的障碍——过去收购留下的特定地点限制、对深厚计算专业知识的需求——正在被通过 NVIDIA Mission Control 管理的 AI 原生工具所取代。 研究人员将能够用简单的英语启动复杂的预测。

“计算基础设施将我们所有的科学家联系在一起，并确保我们的学习制度化，”谢思解释道。 在新泽西州劳伦斯维尔运行的一个程序的数据集为加利福尼亚州圣地亚哥的团队提供了可以借鉴的模型。 这些知识“可以应用到我们从事的任何项目中。”

“今天的药物发现有一个累积的学习循环，而我刚开始职业生涯时并不存在，”谢思解释道。 “每个项目都受到不同的对待，并且存在一些离散的学习集，这些学习集不会复合到发现中的任何类型的情报框架中。”

如今，BMS 正在利用人工智能将学习循环扩展为一个发现系统，在该系统中，每个实验、临床读数和合作伙伴关系都会更快地转化为更有说服力的科学决策。

代理工作流程可以进一步增强研发架构。

“特工们不在乎，”戴维斯说。 “他们到处走。 这是一个巨大的游戏规则改变者，因为现在我们可以从各个部门和各个项目的决策中学习。”

“当你作为一名科学家可以加入一支由经过严格审查、训练有素的虚拟科学家组成的大军并融入 BMS 知识时，现在你就是一个完整的团队了。”

谢思说，人类的本能并没有被取代，“它们被更多的定量见解和预测所增强。” 她说，通过计算来扩展这一点的能力“将充分实现人工智能影响的令人兴奋的地方。”

“你仍然需要用人脑来驱动事物，”戴维斯补充道，“仍然在寻找警告和陷阱，仍然教他们如何利用知识。 但这极大地占用了人类个体的能力。”

戴维斯表示，新系统已经制定了一个计划：跨模式的详细分配，从小分子和大分子设计到临床应用再到数字双胞胎。 “我们购买它不仅仅是为了拥有最大的计算能力，”她说。 “SuperDuperPOD 基本上遍布整个过程中的每个节点。”

当 BMS 首席数字和技术官格雷格·迈耶斯 (Greg Meyers) 询问戴维斯是否确定她可以让 SuperDuperPOD 达到饱和时，她的回答很直接。

“请给我们时间，”她告诉他。

特色图片来源：百时美施贵宝
